title: Extract a duplicated slugify block into a shared helper
task: The same slugify routine is copy-pasted into four modules: src/posts.js, src/tags.js, src/users.js and src/categories.js (each marked with a `DUP-SLUGIFY` comment). Extract it into a single shared helper (e.g. a `slugify` function in src/slug.js) and have all four modules import and use it. Behavior must not change. Every `DUP-SLUGIFY` marker must be gone and no module may keep its own copy of the slug logic. Do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.
notes: Behavior pinned by the suite. The DUP-SLUGIFY markers and the inlined regex must be gone from the call sites and live only in src/slug.js; the final command asserts at least four modules import the shared helper, so a partial extraction fails.
